text: Cybercrime complaints in India should be filed at: (1) cybercrime.gov.in — National Cybercrime Reporting Portal for all cyber crimes; (2) Local police cyber cell; (3) Call 1930 — National Cyber Crime Helpline. Common cybercrimes: Online fraud/scam, UPI fraud, phishing, social media hacking, sextortion, cyberbullying, OTP fraud, fake job offers, investment scams. For UPI fraud: immediately call your bank helpline to freeze the account, then file complaint on cybercrime.gov.in. Time is critical — the sooner you report, the better chance of recovering money.

Whoever intentionally or knowingly captures, publishes or transmits the image of a private area of any person without his or her consent, under circumstances violating the privacy of that person, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to three years or with fine not exceeding two lakh rupees or with both. This covers: secretly filming in changing rooms/bathrooms, non-consensual sharing of intimate images (revenge porn), installing spy cameras without consent. Victims of non-consensual intimate image sharing can also approach the Cyber Crime portal for emergency removal of content.

Section 67 — Publishing obscene material in electronic form: imprisonment up to 3 years (first offence), 5 years (subsequent offence) with fine. Section 67A — Publishing sexually explicit material: imprisonment up to 5 years (first offence), 7 years (subsequent). Section 67B — Child pornography: imprisonment up to 5 years (first offence), 7 years (subsequent) with fine. Reporting: Report child sexual abuse material to cybercrime.gov.in immediately. NCPCR (National Commission for Protection of Child Rights) can also be approached.

section: Section 4 — Grounds for Processing Personal Data
chapter: Chapter II — Obligations of Data Fiduciary
text: The DPDP Act 2023 regulates how organisations collect and use your personal data. As a data principal (person whose data is collected), you have these rights: (1) Right to access information about your data; (2) Right to correction and erasure of data; (3) Right to grievance redressal; (4) Right to nominate another person to exercise rights on your behalf. Organisations must take your consent before collecting personal data. They must tell you what data is collected and why. Data Breach must be reported to the Data Protection Board.

act_name: Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules 2021
section: Rule 7 — Grievance Officer of Social Media Platforms
text: Every significant social media intermediary (WhatsApp, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter/X, YouTube, etc.) must appoint a Grievance Officer based in India. You can file complaint with the Grievance Officer for: content removal, harassment, misinformation about you, hacking of your account. The Grievance Officer must acknowledge complaint within 24 hours and resolve within 15 days. If not resolved, you can appeal to the Grievance Appellate Committee (GAC) at gac.gov.in. For emergency content removal (affecting safety/dignity), social media platforms must act within 24 hours.
